features.summary: Broadcom reported record Q4 2024 consolidated revenue of $14.1 billion, up 51% year-over-year, driven by strong AI demand and VMware integration. AI revenue grew 220% to $12.2 billion for fiscal 2024, representing 41% of semiconductor revenue. The VMware acquisition integration is largely complete with operating margins reaching 70%. For Q1 2025, the company guides consolidated revenue to $14.6 billion with AI revenue expected at $3.8 billion, up 65% year-over-year.
features.management_outlook: Management projects a massive AI opportunity with a serviceable addressable market of $60-90 billion by fiscal 2027 for their three hyperscale customers deploying 1 million XPU clusters. They expect AI semiconductor business to rapidly outgrow non-AI segments and will now guide by AI versus non-AI revenue segments. Two additional hyperscale customers are in advanced development for next-generation AI XPUs. Non-AI semiconductor business is expected to recover at mid-single digit growth rates from the cyclical bottom.
features.risks: The company faces quarterly variability in AI revenue due to having only three hyperscale customers and deployment coming in large chunks rather than linear growth. Higher cash taxes are expected due to increased U.S. taxable income mix and regulatory changes. Gross margins in semiconductors may face pressure from higher AI XPU mix, though this should be offset by operating leverage from revenue growth.
features.key_drivers: AI demand remains the primary growth driver with custom AI accelerators (XPUs) and networking connectivity showing strong momentum. VMware's focus on data center virtualization with 70% of bookings from VMware Cloud Foundation is driving infrastructure software growth. The company expects AI networking content to increase from 5-10% today to 15-20% as clusters scale to 500,000-1 million XPUs, with next-generation 3nm XPUs launching in H2 2025.
